title: Emergency Fund Target Calculator
metaDescription: Calculate how much you should keep in an emergency fund based on your monthly expenses, instantly in your browser.
shortDescription: Calculate your target emergency fund size based on monthly expenses.

guideSections.heading: Why the Right Buffer Period Depends on Income Stability, Not a Fixed Rule
guideSections.body: Three months of expenses is a commonly cited minimum, but it assumes relatively stable, predictable income. Commission-based work, freelancing, or a single-income household with dependents typically warrants a larger buffer — six to twelve months — since the cost of an income gap compounds when there's less flexibility to absorb it.

guideSections.heading: Why This Should Be Based on Expenses, Not Income
guideSections.body: Sizing an emergency fund against income rather than actual monthly expenses overstates what's needed for someone with a high savings rate and understates it for someone living closer to their full income. The fund exists to cover real spending during a gap, which makes expenses the more accurate basis for the calculation.
